D9 BiologyL4 AnalyzeReference

Catshark Fluorescence — Skin Glows Green at Depth

chain catshark skin absorbs the abundant blue (450 nm) below 200 m depth and emits 525 nm green via a previously unknown family of small-molecule fluorophores (Park 2019)

Park et al. 2019 identified novel brominated tryptophan-kynurenine derivatives in chain catshark (Scyliorhinus retifer) skin. They absorb blue and emit green — same idea as GFP but using small molecules, not protein chromophores. The fluorescence is visible to other sharks (whose vision is shifted into the blue) but invisible to predators.

Toggle deep-blue light to reveal the green skin pattern only visible under those conditions.
